birthstone_status: June birthstone (modern — alongside pearl and moonstone)
search_boost_terms: emerald by day ruby by night
_all_text: Alexandrite alexandrite BeAl2O4 (with Cr3+) The alexandrite effect requires Cr3+ in an orthorhombic field (different from trigonal corundum). Two broad absorption bands in the blue-yellow region and a transmission window in both the red and green-blue create a near-balance that shifts depending on illuminant. Tungsten light emphasizes red transmission; daylight/fluorescent light emphasizes blue-green. Vanadium also contributes to color change in some specimens. Alexandrite is unique — a single stone with two identities. Under northern daylight or fluorescent light it is distinctly green; under incandescent (candle, tungsten) light it turns vivid red-purple. The best Russian specimens shift from 'emerald' to 'ruby' — an impossible color change nicknamed 'the emerald by day, ruby by night' gem. No other natural gemstone produces such a dramatic and complete color change. Alexandrite was officially discovered on April 17, 1834 — the 16th birthday of Crown Prince Alexander II of Russia, who would later become Tsar. The stone was named in his honor by mineralogist Nils Gustaf Nordenskiöld. Its green-and-red color change matched the military colors of Imperial Russia, making it doubly auspicious. Russian jewelers favored it highly until the Revolution. Until the 1950s, alexandrite was one of the rarest colored gemstones; Brazilian deposits have made it more available, though fine Russian stones remain extreme rarities. duality and transformation good luck and balanced opposing forces Imperial Russia mystery and hidden nature June and summer solstice Gemstone — discovered 1834 in Ural Mountains Russian imperial gem during Romanov dynasty Tsar Alexander II's stone — named on his 16th birthday (April 17, 1834) Art Nouveau jewelry piece (late 19th century) alexandrite color change gem chrysoberyl alexandrite emerald by day ruby by night Ural alexandrite June birthstone BeAl2O4 chromium alexandrite oxide variety chrysoberyl gemstone color-change birthstone june orthorhombic beryllium high-priority rare color-change-duality imperial-Russian emerald-by-day ruby-by-night chromium-paradox green (daylight) red-purple (incandescent light)
